name: UAE Cybersecurity & Data Protection Compliance Registry
note: This file is the structured data layer of the UAE Regulations Registry. Rich analysis, penalty detail, and operational guidance are delivered privately via email / LinkedIn DM / WhatsApp — see README.md 'Full analysis' section.

regulations.key_requirements: Data collection/processing rules; purpose limitation; data minimisation; data subject rights (access, erasure, portability); mandatory DPIAs for high-risk processing; breach notification to Data Office; cross-border transfer restrictions; consent management.
regulations.compliance_deadline: In force since 2 Jan 2022 (no fixed deadline)
regulations.deadline_notes: PDPL in force since 2 Jan 2022; once Executive Regulations publish, 6-month compliance window per Art. 56
regulations.enforcement_status: In force; full enforcement pending Executive Regulations
regulations.max_penalty: Penalties pending Executive Regulations (legal analyses cite AED 50K – AED 10M)

regulations.key_requirements: Criminal liability for unauthorized system access; data breaches; identity theft; electronic fraud; phishing; ransomware; blackmail. Executive criminal liability for negligence leading to breaches.
regulations.max_penalty: AED 100K – 3M per offense + imprisonment; up to AED 2M for paid content dissemination

regulations.applies_to_sectors: Finance, Health, Retail, Telecom, Logistics, Education
regulations.key_requirements: Security-by-design mandate; periodic cyber risk assessments; incident response plans; ISO 27001 alignment; vendor/third-party risk management; IoT and cloud security standards; mandatory MFA on critical systems.
regulations.deadline_notes: Sector-specific enforcement phases; critical infra sectors first
regulations.max_penalty: No direct NCS penalties (enforced via PDPL, CII provisions, sector regs)
regulations.breach_notification: Per underlying laws (PDPL ER, ADHICS, CBUAE, sectoral)

regulations.key_requirements: GDPR-aligned: DPIAs mandatory; annual DPO review; documented adequacy assessments for cross-border transfers; private right of action (individuals may sue in DIFC Courts); data minimisation; breach notification.
regulations.deadline_notes: 2025 amendments effective immediately — no grace period
regulations.max_penalty: USD 25K (annual assessment failure); USD 50K (DPIA failure); USD 50K (data sharing/disclosure); plus pre-existing Art. 62 fines

regulations.key_requirements: Cyber risk governance; senior executive accountability for cyber; cyber risk assessments; controls (access mgmt, encryption, network security, monitoring, incident response, BCM, third-party); GEN 5.5.19 mandatory cyber incident reporting to DFSA.
regulations.deadline_notes: GEN Module 5.5 effective 1 Jan 2024 — distinct from DIFC DPL (different regulator)
regulations.max_penalty: DFSA enforcement action + fines under DFSA Regulatory Law
regulations.data_localization: N/A (DFSA cyber rules; data residency via DIFC DPL)
regulations.breach_notification: GEN 5.5.19 — mandatory cyber incident notification to DFSA

regulations.key_requirements: Privacy-by-design; DPO mandatory; SCCs/BCRs for international transfers; 72-hour breach notification to regulator + data subject notification if required; active administrative enforcement.
regulations.deadline_notes: Active enforcement — 2 public regulatory actions on record (Okadoc 21 May 2024, VentureRock 23 Jun 2023); fine amounts not disclosed publicly; statutory max USD 28M
regulations.max_penalty: Up to USD 28M (statutory cap; no fine amounts publicly disclosed)

regulations.key_requirements: Customer financial data protection; payment data localization (must remain in UAE); 5-year data retention for payment services; 72-hour incident notification for card scheme failures; open finance and virtual-asset payment cybersecurity.
regulations.deadline_notes: Effective 16 Sep 2025 — Art. 184 reconciliation deadline 16 Sep 2026 (CBUAE Board may extend)
regulations.max_penalty: License revocation + administrative fines up to 10× violation value or unjust enrichment

regulations.key_requirements: Full patient health data localization; end-to-end encryption for PHI; role-based EMR access controls; cybersecurity monitoring; regular security audits; DPIA for new health tech deployments. Cross-border exemptions renewable annually.
regulations.deadline_notes: v2.0 May 2024 supersedes v1.0 (Feb 2019); Basic controls deadline Nov 2024 (passed); Advanced May 2025 (passed); cross-border exemptions 1yr renewable via DoH AAMEN portal

regulations.short_reference: DHA Policies & Standards + DHA Policy HISHD/PP-13 (Aug 2024, eff. 10 Nov 2024)
regulations.key_requirements: Patient data confidentiality; secure health information exchange; PHI localization; cybersecurity standards for EMRs; audit trails for all patient data access; PDPL compliance for consent flows.
regulations.deadline_notes: DHA Policy HISHD/PP-13 effective 10 Nov 2024; mandatory for ALL DHA-licensed facilities; DHA Circular CIR-2025-0000954 requires NABIDH-integrated EMRs by 31 Dec 2025
regulations.breach_notification: 24-48 hours to UAE Information Office + DHA (datacompliance@dha.gov.ae)

regulations.regulation: Federal Health Data Law (Use of ICT in Health Fields)
regulations.short_reference: Federal Law No. 2/2019 + Cabinet Res. 32/2020 (Executive Reg) + Min. Decision 51/2021 (cross-border categories)
regulations.key_requirements: Mandatory health data localization — all electronic health records must be stored in UAE. Cross-border transfer of patient data prohibited except with explicit authority approval. Strict confidentiality of patient information.
regulations.deadline_notes: Cross-border transfers require case-by-case ministerial approval
regulations.max_penalty: AED 1,000–1,000,000 per violation + license suspension/cancellation + UAE Penal Code exposure

regulations.applies_to_sectors: Finance — Crypto, NFT, Virtual Asset exchanges
regulations.key_requirements: Anti-hacking infrastructure; secure crypto wallet systems; AML/CFT compliance; mandatory cybersecurity audits; incident reporting; secure custody of digital assets; data protection for trading activity records.
regulations.deadline_notes: T&I Rulebook eff. 19 May 2025; full compliance from 19 Jun 2025; annual TLPT/Red Team; quarterly vulnerability scans; continuous monitoring
regulations.data_localization: UAE data protection laws apply; secure key/seed backup in separate locations (no Dubai-only mandate confirmed)

regulations.regulating_body: Child Digital Safety Council (chaired by Minister of Family) + TDRA (ISP supervision)
regulations.applies_to_sectors: ALL digital platforms + ISPs (search, social, gaming, streaming, apps, messaging, VOD, e-commerce) — extraterritorial: any platform directed to UAE users
regulations.key_requirements: Under-13: verifiable parental consent. Under-18: age verification, no behavioural profiling, no targeted ads, content filtering, parental controls, PDPL-aligned children's data.
regulations.deadline_notes: Effective 1 Jan 2026 — full compliance deadline 1 Jan 2027
regulations.max_penalty: Platform suspension + fines (amounts set by Cabinet implementing regulations)

regulations.short_reference: FL 3/2003 (Telecom Law) + UAE Information Assurance Regulation v1.1 (2020) + UAE National Cloud Security Policy (2023) + TDRA Cloud First Policy + TDRA CPR
regulations.applies_to_sectors: Licensed telecom operators, ISPs, cloud service providers, government entities using cloud (Logistics/Retail captured indirectly as customers)
regulations.key_requirements: Mandatory UAE data residency for telecom customer data; cloud security standards; content filtering obligations for ISPs; cybersecurity audit requirements for cloud providers; no offshore hosting without TDRA approval.
regulations.deadline_notes: Data must remain in UAE — no offshore hosting without TDRA approval
regulations.max_penalty: Fines per implementing TDRA/Cabinet decisions + license suspension/revocation
